evals.prompt: What's the latest stable Go release right now, and when was it released? I want sources I can verify.
evals.expected_output: answer states a concrete Go version number (e.g. 1.26.x); answer includes at least one clickable https:// source link; tool_calls.json records at least one google_search call with a non-empty self-contained query
assertions: answer states a concrete Go version number (e.g. 1.26.x)
answer includes at least one clickable https:// source link
tool_calls.json records at least one google_search call with a non-empty self-contained query

evals.prompt: Read https://go.dev/doc/devel/release and tell me which Go release the newest entry covers, and summarize its headline changes.
evals.expected_output: at least one google_search call sets url_context=true (the query embeds a URL to open); some call's query contains the go.dev/doc/devel/release URL; answer names a specific Go release (Go 1.x); answer includes at least one https:// source link
assertions: at least one google_search call sets url_context=true (the query embeds a URL to open)
some call's query contains the go.dev/doc/devel/release URL
answer names a specific Go release (Go 1.x)
answer includes at least one https:// source link

evals.prompt: Find the current estimated populations of the Tokyo metro area and the Osaka (Keihanshin) metro area, and compute the Tokyo/Osaka ratio to two decimal places. Cite sources.
evals.expected_output: at least one google_search call sets code_execution=true OR the answer shows an explicitly computed ratio with two decimals; answer contains a numeric ratio with two decimal places; answer includes at least one https:// source link
assertions: at least one google_search call sets code_execution=true OR the answer shows an explicitly computed ratio with two decimals
answer contains a numeric ratio with two decimal places
answer includes at least one https:// source link
